body: PT Bakrie & Brothers Tbk (BNBR) has reduced its total liabilities by 19.07% to IDR 3.62 trillion after a debt-to-equity conversion worth IDR 855 billion, resulting in a liabilities-to-equity ratio improvement from 1.62 to 1.0. The company's current assets have increased by 130.28%, and its market capitalization has risen to IDR 7.97 trillion, with its stock price improving by IDR 25 per share over the last six months.
title: PT Bakrie & Brothers reduces total liabilities by 19.07% through debt-to-equity conversion.

body: PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi Tbk (EMTK) increased its stake in PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (SCMA) to 61.32% through a IDR 18 billion transaction, acquiring 100 million additional shares at IDR 180 each, representing 0.14% of SCMA's issued shares. Following the transaction, SCMA's shares rose 6.18% to IDR 191 per share, with EMTK's subsidiary seeing a 41.48% price increase over the past week.
title: PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi increases stake in PT Surya Citra Media to 61.32% through IDR 18 billion transaction.

body: PT Remala Abadi TBK (REM) aims to reach IDR 412.91 billion in revenue by 2025, a 10% increase from its 2024 forecast, driven by a focus on growing its retail segment and building a new network of 250,000 home connections. The company is optimistic about achieving its revenue target, with a net profit margin expected to increase to 21.54% and EBITDA to 45.09% in 2025, up from 19.31% and 44.02% in 2024, respectively.
title: PT Remala Abadi targets 10% revenue growth to IDR 412.91 billion in 2025.

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index (JCI) fell 0.74% or 55.24 points to 7,409.51 in the first trading session, with 9 out of 11 sectoral indices declining, led by Technology (-0.99%) and Finance (-0.89%). Meanwhile, PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) and PT Era Digital Media Tbk were among the top-traded shares, with PT Trust Finance Indonesia Tbk (TRUST) being the top gainer with a 25% share price increase.
title: Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index Falls 0.74% in First Trading Session Today

body: PT Sejahteraraya Anugerahjaya Tbk (SRAJ) saw its stock price decline by IDR 50 to IDR 2,490 after announcing a plan to issue a USD 125 million bond, despite a 730% surge in its stock price year-to-date. The company's stock has slowed down 27.42% from IDR 3,500 last month, but remains up significantly from IDR 306 per share on January 2, 2024.
title: PT Sejahteraraya Anugerahjaya's stock corrects after surging 730% this year despite plans to issue USD 125 million bond.

body: Foreign investors purchased a net 315.21 million shares of Indonesian issuers, with PT Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOJEK) topping the buy list with 536.12 million shares, followed by PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastuktur Tbk (ASTR) with 118.09 million shares. On the sell side, foreign investors released a net 295 million shares of PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (SCMA), followed by PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) with 140 million shares, and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with 51.32 million shares.
title: Foreign investors purchase 6.68 billion shares of Indonesian issuers, led by PT Gojek Tokopedia and PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur.

body: PT Sejahteraraya Anugrahjaya Tbk (SRAJ) plans to issue bonds worth USD 125 million to fund working capital requirements for its subsidiaries, including Mayapada Apollo Batam International Hospital and Mayapada Hospital Surabaya. The bond proceeds will be allocated to various subsidiaries, including PT Nirmala Kencana Mas Development, PT Anugrah Inti Bahagia Construction, and others, with strategic investors BCCS Maverick Holdings I II, LP, and Bain Capital Credit, LP participating in the plan.
title: Mayapada Hospital Operator to Issue USD 125 Million Bonds to Fund Subsidiaries' Working Capital Requirements

body: According to Bank Indonesia's retail sales survey, the price expectation index is projected to rise to 157.8 in January 2025 before declining to 165.4 in April 2025, driven by seasonal factors such as rainfall and holiday demand. Retail sales are expected to decrease to 144.7 in January 2025 and 146.5 in April 2025, down from 163.9 in December 2024, due to demand normalisation after holidays.
title: Indonesian inflation expected to rise in January 2025 before declining in April 2025 due to seasonal factors.

body: Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation (OCBC) and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd (CIMB) have submitted non-binding offers to become the controlling shareholder of PT Bank Pan Indonesia Tbk (Bank Panin), with the deal expected to be completed by the end of the year, which would boost Bank Panin's market capitalization to IDR 47.91 trillion. Bank Panin's shares have surged 77.88% to IDR 2,010 per share since the beginning of the year, with the acquisition allowing OCBC and CIMB to consolidate Bank Panin into their respective business units in Indonesia.
title: OCBC and CIMB Group Holdings consider acquiring Bank Panin, submitting non-binding offers to become controlling shareholders.

body: PT Chitose Internasional Tbk (CINT) saw a 34% increase in its stock price, reaching IDR 240 per share, its highest record year-to-date, after a steady uptrend since morning. PT Haloni Jane Tbk also experienced a 34% jump, but its price remained lower than CINT's, placing it at the runner-up spot, with a share price of IDR 67.
title: PT Chitose Internasional TBK Sees 34% Price Surge to Reach Highest Record This Year

body: PT XL Axiata Tbk and PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk have signed a definitive agreement for a merger worth USD 6.5 billion (IDR 104 trillion), which will officially take effect on March 22, 2025, and position Anchor and Stellar as controlling parties. The merger will result in a new brand name, PT XLSmart Telecom Sejahtera Tbk, with an effective date of April 15, 2025.
title: XL Axiata and Smartfren Telecom to Merge Under New Name PT XLSmart Telecom Sejahtera TBK.

body: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BUMI) announced a mineral reserve report revealing 3.54 million ounces of gold reserves with a grade of 3.2 grams per ton, with 84% coming from underground mining prospects, particularly the River Reef area with a high gold grade of 4.9 g/t. The company plans to begin mining underground mineral reserves in Palu soon, with its subsidiary PT Citra Palu Minerals appointing PT Macmahon Indonesia, a subsidiary of Macmahon Holdings Limited (MAH), as the mining contractor.
title: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Announces Significant Gold Reserves at River Reef Hill Reef Mining Sites in Palu.

body: The Jakarta Composite Index (IHSG) rose 0.21% to 7,453.29 on Tuesday, following a 0.74% increase on Monday, driven by consistent net buying from foreign investors worth IDR 84.26 billion (USD 5.31 million) on Tuesday and IDR 296.35 billion (USD 18.56 million) on Monday. PT Sumber Alfaria Trijaya Tbk led the top gainers, while PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk was the top loser, with 116 stocks posting gains over 2% and 198 stocks declining up to 2%.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Rises for Second Consecutive Day with Foreign Investors Driving Net Buying Activity

body: Fore Coffee plans to open 216 outlets across 43 cities in Indonesia, driven by the promising prospects of the country's coffee industry, which is expected to increase coffee consumption by 4.8 million bags in 2024/2025 from 4.45 million bags in 2020/2021. The company's digital platform, launched in 2018, has been downloaded by millions of users, providing a personal interactive experience and contributing to the growth of the industry, which still faces challenges such as low per capita coffee consumption of 1 kilogram per year.
title: Fore Coffee Expands to 216 Outlets Across 43 Cities in Indonesia Amid Growing Coffee Consumption.

body: The Indonesian government aims to accelerate economic growth to 8% over the next five years, exceeding projected growth rates of 5.1% in 2024 and 5.2% in 2025, by boosting consumer spending and domestic investment, while maintaining inflation at a manageable level. To achieve this, the government is implementing initiatives such as national shopping days, tourism promotions, and special economic zones, which have already attracted IDR 242.5 trillion in investment and created 151,260 jobs.
title: Indonesian Government Unveils Strategies to Boost Economic Growth to 8% Over Next Five Years
